name: rollup_definitions
title: Rollup Definitions
description: Declared aggregations (plan/vocabulary/14-rollup-spec.md): one record per definition -- a source collection, an optional flat-equality filter, a group_by, an optional time_bucket, and a list of metrics. The daemon's rollup pass (object_daemon.process_rollups, object_rollups.compute_rollup) materializes the answer into target_collection, an ordinary schema-shaped collection generated from this definition. filter/group_by/metrics are stored as JSON text -- this schema does not parse or validate their structure; object_rollups.parse_definition does that at compute time, same convention app-finance's fin_recurring.template_lines uses for its own JSON-shaped field. Admin-owned, full stop (see permissions/rules.json): a definition's filter/group-by shape reveals source schema structure.

fields.name: source_collection
fields.label: Source Collection
fields.help: Exactly one collection -- no joins. The rollup pass reads it outside row-level permissions (daemon posture), so it must already exist.

fields.name: filter
fields.label: Filter (JSON)
fields.help: Optional flat {field: value} object, ANDed on equality -- e.g. {"status": "paid"}. No OR, no sub-query, no cross-collection join, no computed expression; a report that needs one of those is a custom object, not a rollup definition. Blank means every row.

fields.name: group_by
fields.label: Group By (JSON list)
fields.help: JSON list of source field names, e.g. ["channel"]. Empty or blank means one row total per bucket.

fields.name: time_bucket
fields.label: Time Bucket (JSON)
fields.help: Optional {"field": <source date/datetime field>, "granularity": none|day|week|month} object, e.g. {"field": "created_at", "granularity": "day"}. granularity defaults to "none" (bucket_start present, unbucketed) when omitted. Blank means no time dimension at all -- bucket_start is absent from the target. No timezone conversion: truncation happens in the field's own stored value, matching docs/schema-forms.md's date/datetime types.

fields.name: metrics
fields.label: Metrics (JSON list)
fields.help: JSON list of {"op": count|sum|min|max|avg, "field": <source field, required except for count>, "as": <target field name, optional>}. avg is always sum/count computed fresh from that group's exact values, never a running average.

fields.name: target_collection
fields.label: Target Collection
fields.help: Where the computed answer lands -- an ordinary collection whose schema is generated from this definition and regenerated on every recompute. Dedicated 1:1 to this definition; do not point two definitions at the same target.

fields.name: min_group_size
fields.label: Minimum Group Size
fields.help: Groups (within a bucket) with fewer underlying rows than this are suppressed -- dropped from the target entirely, never merged into an "other" bucket. Blank means no suppression. See permissions/rules.json's reason field for the disclosure threat this exists to blunt: it is declared, not enforced against whatever permission rule the target collection is later granted.

fields.name: refresh_mode
fields.label: Refresh Mode
fields.help: scheduled (recommended default) recomputes the whole definition every refresh_interval_seconds. incremental is accepted but v1's daemon pass has no create-fast-path yet -- an incremental definition simply behaves as scheduled-only until that lands (plan/vocabulary/14-rollup-spec.md's Events section).

fields.name: refresh_interval_seconds
fields.label: Refresh Interval (seconds)
fields.help: Required in both refresh modes -- the scheduled recompute interval, or (once incremental's fast path exists) the reconciling recompute interval that runs alongside it.

fields.name: last_computed_at
fields.label: Last Computed
fields.help: Server-set by the rollup pass after a successful recompute. This is both the freshness signal and the scheduled-mode due gate: a definition is due once refresh_interval_seconds have elapsed since this value (or immediately, if it has never run).

fields.name: enabled
fields.label: Enabled
fields.help: Per-definition kill switch, independent of the instance-wide rollup_enabled flag -- pause one noisy or broken definition without disabling every rollup.
